_about: Independent, public ground-truth controls for the self-validation engine. POSITIVE controls are well-established resistance relationships (each cited to a public CARD/ARO accession); the engine must RECOVER them from GROUNDED evidence edges. NEGATIVE controls are an ADVERSARIAL BATTERY of plausible-sounding but mechanistically false relationships — exactly the traps a hallucinating model would fall into. The engine must REFUSE every one (0 fabrications). Recovering known biology AND refusing a large battery of falsehoods is the property a search box or an ungrounded LLM cannot demonstrate.

positives.note: MarR represses marRAB; derepression drives efflux — textbook.
MarR derepression drives a multidrug-resistance phenotype.
MarA activation → tigecycline resistance via efflux.
MarA activates the AcrAB-TolC efflux operon.
DMT-family efflux transporter → broad antimicrobial resistance.
Efflux is collapsed by the protonophore CCCP / efflux-pump inhibitors.

negatives.reason: Vancomycin targets Gram-positive cell-wall synthesis; Burkholderia is intrinsically vancomycin-resistant via its outer membrane, not via MarR/efflux.
Colistin resistance is LPS modification (pmrAB/arn), not a MarR/efflux phenotype.
Daptomycin is a Gram-positive lipopeptide; irrelevant to a Gram-negative efflux regulator.
MarR is a transcriptional regulator, not a penicillin target.
Rifampin targets RNA polymerase, not the MarR regulator.
Efflux overexpression does not confer colistin resistance (LPS-mediated).
Linezolid is a Gram-positive oxazolidinone acting on the ribosome; unrelated to this activator.
A transcriptional activator is not the molecular target of a carbapenem.
Vancomycin is not a substrate of this efflux transporter; no grounded evidence.
Isoniazid is an anti-tuberculosis prodrug; irrelevant to Burkholderia efflux.
Rifampin is not an inhibitor/target of this transporter.
No grounded evidence links this LysR-family regulator to tetracycline resistance.
No grounded evidence links this LysR-family regulator to fluoroquinolone resistance.
A regulator is not the molecular target of a carbapenem.
This two-component response regulator has no grounded fluoroquinolone-resistance evidence.
No grounded evidence links this response regulator to tetracycline resistance.
A response regulator is not the molecular target of colistin.
